on several benchmarks. We compare it with state-

5.1  Language Modeling
Setup.  We evaluate the PG-19 dataset to measu
PPL under context lengths of up to 32k tokens.
lower PPL indicates that the model is less surpris
by ground truth tokens, reflecting higher confiden
in its predictions. In our setup, the model is fed s
quences exceeding 32k tokens from the dataset, f
lowed by autoregressive decoding, during which PP
is computed at each step. Lower PPL values cor
spond to better predictive performance.

6.1  Sentence chunking
An alternative strategy to our sentence-level
T
chunking  is  to perform equal-sized chunk-
ing  based  on  the  total  number  of  sen-
tences—dividing the text uniformly. However,
this approach consistently yields worse results
on LongBench (see Table 2), likely due to the
loss of coherence and topical alignment within
chunks. When sentences are split arbitrarily, im-
portant semantic relationships and narrative flow
